recipes.name: Tofu Scramble with Spinach
recipes.description: High-protein breakfast scramble loaded with iron and complete amino acids.
steps: Drain and press tofu for 10 minutes, then crumble into bite-sized pieces.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat with a drizzle of oil.
Add crumbled tofu and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly golden.
Add diced bell pepper and cook for 2 minutes.
Add spinach and cook 2-3 minutes until wilted.
Sprinkle nutritional yeast, stir well, season with salt, pepper, and turmeric. Serve warm.

recipes.name: Overnight Oats with Chia & Banana
recipes.description: No-cook breakfast packed with slow-release energy and omega-3s.
steps: Combine oats, chia seeds, and milk in a jar or bowl. Stir well.
Cover and refrigerate overnight (or at least 4 hours).
In the morning, slice the banana and layer on top.
Stir gently and enjoy cold, or microwave 1-2 minutes if preferred warm.

recipes.name: Lentil Soup with Kale
recipes.description: Hearty, iron-rich soup perfect for recovery days.
steps: Peel and dice sweet potato into 1 cm cubes.
In a large pot, sauté sweet potato in a little oil over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Add 600 ml of water or vegetable broth and bring to a boil.
Add lentils, reduce heat, and simmer for 15 minutes until sweet potato is tender.
Stir in chopped kale and cook 3-4 minutes until wilted.
Remove from heat, dissolve miso paste in a small amount of warm broth, then stir into the soup. Serve hot.

recipes.name: Chickpea Quinoa Power Bowl
recipes.description: Complete protein bowl with all essential amino acids covered.
steps: Warm quinoa and chickpeas together in a pan or microwave.
Wilt spinach in a pan for 2 minutes with a pinch of salt.
Assemble bowls: layer quinoa, then chickpeas, then spinach.
Slice avocado and arrange on top.
Drizzle tahini over the bowl. Season with lemon juice, salt, and pepper.

recipes.name: Tempeh Stir-Fry with Brown Rice
recipes.description: Savory stir-fry with fermented soy protein and sesame crunch.
steps: Slice tempeh into thin strips or cubes.
Heat oil in a wok or large pan over high heat.
Sear tempeh strips for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and crispy.
Add broccoli florets and sliced mushrooms. Stir-fry 4-5 minutes.
Add soy sauce (2 tbsp) and a splash of rice vinegar. Toss to coat.
Serve over brown rice and sprinkle with sesame seeds.

recipes.name: Black Bean Burrito Bowl
recipes.description: Tex-Mex inspired bowl loaded with fiber and plant protein.
steps: Warm black beans in a pan with cumin, chili powder, and a pinch of salt.
Dice bell pepper into strips.
Assemble bowls: layer brown rice, then seasoned black beans.
Top with sliced avocado and diced bell pepper.
Grate cheddar cheese over the top. Add salsa or hot sauce to taste.

recipes.name: Peanut Butter Banana Toast
recipes.description: Quick energy-dense snack ideal before morning training.
steps: Toast two slices of whole wheat bread until golden.
Spread peanut butter evenly on each slice.
Slice banana and layer on top of peanut butter.
Sprinkle hemp seeds over the banana slices. Serve immediately.

recipes.name: Greek Yogurt Parfait
recipes.description: Creamy, crunchy parfait rich in protein and antioxidants.
steps: Spoon half the Greek yogurt into a glass or bowl.
Add a layer of oats and half the blueberries.
Add the remaining yogurt on top.
Finish with remaining blueberries and roughly chopped almonds.

recipes.name: Recovery Protein Smoothie
recipes.description: Post-workout smoothie for rapid muscle recovery.
steps: Add soy milk and banana to a blender.
Add whey protein powder and peanut butter.
Blend on high for 30-45 seconds until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and drink within 30 minutes of finishing your workout.

recipes.name: Trail Mix Energy Balls
recipes.description: No-bake portable snack perfect for trail days.
steps: Pit dates and soak in warm water for 5 minutes, then drain.
Blend dates in a food processor until a sticky paste forms.
In a bowl, combine oats, peanut butter, date paste, and ground flaxseed.
Chop dark chocolate into small chips and fold into the mixture.
Roll into 12 balls (about 25g each). Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 5 days.

recipes.name: Pasta Primavera
recipes.description: Veggie-loaded whole wheat pasta with creamy cottage cheese sauce.
steps: Cook whole wheat pasta according to package directions. Reserve 100 ml pasta water before draining.
While pasta cooks, dice bell pepper and slice zucchini into half-moons.
Sauté bell pepper and zucchini in olive oil for 4-5 minutes over medium-high heat.
Add broccoli florets and cook 2 more minutes.
Toss drained pasta with the vegetables.
Stir in cottage cheese and a splash of pasta water to create a light creamy sauce. Season with salt, pepper, and Italian herbs.

recipes.name: Edamame Miso Bowl
recipes.description: Japanese-inspired grain bowl with umami-rich miso dressing.
steps: Warm buckwheat groats in a pan or microwave.
Steam or boil edamame for 3-4 minutes until tender.
Wilt kale in a pan with a splash of water for 2-3 minutes.
Make miso dressing: whisk miso paste with 2 tbsp warm water, 1 tsp sesame oil, and 1 tsp rice vinegar.
Assemble bowls: layer buckwheat, edamame, and kale.
Drizzle miso dressing over the bowl and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
